title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledges truth to criticisms about his left hand after past injuries affected his game.
Brown underwent major surgery on his left wrist in 2021 and reinjured it in 2023.
Improved health has helped Brown achieve career-high stats, boosting his performance and MVP candidacy.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returned to Boston to broadcast Padres games, nearly 11 years after his NESN tenure ended.
Orsillo, former Red Sox play-by-play announcer, expressed gratitude for fan support, calling his NESN job a 'dream'.
In Boston, Orsillo reunited with NESN colleagues, sharing fond memories and reflecting on his time with the Red Sox.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox's Roman Anthony is being used frequently as designated hitter due to defensive struggles, says manager Alex Cora.
Anthony contributed offensively against the Padres with a triple but struggled defensively, committing a costly error.
The Red Sox aim to improve their 2-6 record, entering Sunday's game against Padres at Fenway Park.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs over four innings despite walking four batters in a 3-2 loss to the Padres.
Closer Aroldis Chapman gave up the Padres' winning run in the ninth, resulting in Boston's sixth loss of the season.
Early has not allowed a home run in 28.2 innings and has 39 strikeouts, tying a Red Sox record for most in first six games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0, with goals from Langoni, Fofana, and Miller securing the victory.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card in the 34th minute for leaving the technical area.
Peyton Miller, an academy graduate, scored a late goal, continuing his form after missing most of the preseason.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas will resume hitting after rib injury, following a shutdown due to an intercostal strain.
Johan Oviedo is experiencing right elbow strain, will meet with Dr. Keith Meister after reduced pitching velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval, both recovering from past injuries, near starts in rehab assignments.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, Taylor was treated successfully at MGH while maintaining her studies and athletics.
Taylor expresses gratitude to her doctors and the Adolescent and Young Adult program, running to honor fellow cancer patients.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Boston Medical Center's social workers and safety-net mission.
Beattie is raising funds for BMC, aiming to collect $10,000 through events, to aid in crisis patient care.
Inspired by her mother and roommate, Beattie highlights social workers as crucial yet resource-strained in the healthcare system.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium on April 3, continuing their winless streak.
Legacy's defense showed resilience, but offensive struggles persist, with only one goal in four games this season.
Coach Filipa Patão stresses patience, urging players to stay composed in attack and improve accuracy and positioning.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., 57, was charged in Malden District Court with embezzling client funds and unauthorized legal practice.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 entrusted for a client trust account and failed to return it after a legal dispute.
Despite suspensions, Rappa continued practicing law, improperly handling funds at a real estate closing in December 2025.
